title: dianthus × digeneus
intro: Dianthus × digeneus, also known as Sweet William or Fern-Leafed Sweet William, is a hybrid of the Dianthus barbatus and Dianthus plumarius species, belonging to the carnation family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Dianthus × digeneus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Dianthus × digeneus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
